creator: d8zNeCFG@aon.at
text:
Thanks for the explanation of the default MTU.

Yes I have seen that option but thought it would not apply in this case, the "rogue router" being the border router running mpd5 and ipfw: There is nothing which prevents ICMP messages generated by the border router to be received by the internal machine, and similarly I don't think that outside routers would drop ICMP messages generated by the border router.

Also, very seldom it does work - why would that be?

Also, it works when doing the connection from the border router itself.

Could it be that the problem lies with ng_nat: It probably does not correctly alias/de-alias ICMP messages belonging to a certain TCP connection?

Anyway, I'll test your suggestion (might take a little while).

-- Martin
